answer.text: Feel the pain of losses approximately twice as intensely as the pleasure of equivalent gains, leading to irrational holding of losing investments
Never take any risk with their capital
Always sell winning investments too early

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Loss aversion causes investors to hold losing positions too long (hoping to break even) while selling winners too quickly (locking in gains). Losses hurt roughly 2x as much as equivalent gains feel good. **The Trap:** Loss aversion does not mean investors prefer losses. It means the psychological impact of losses is disproportionately larger. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tested in NISM XV Ch 12 and X-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Counter loss aversion with pre-set stop losses and target prices. Remove emotion from sell decisions by committing to rules before investing.
